title: Design System: Hirslanden — Variant B (Care-routing first)

variant.bet: The home opens with 'What do you need today?' intent cards beside search; the flat module stack collapses into one intent-organized layer.
variant.amplifies: Audience-routing reframe (what-if #7) + search-as-tool (#3), in service of Design Principle 2 (search-first wayfinding).
variant.differentiatingToken: intent-card component as page-lead layer; collapsed module stack; sectionPadding 64px
variant.motion: static — gentle hover/focus on intent cards only; NO motion register

hirslanden-blue.note: marks the four wayfinding intent cards + search

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Shared across the five intent cards so the routing layer reads as one organized choice.

header.purpose: Wordmark + primary nav + search + utility bar; reachable emergency path.

footer.purpose: Group blurb, social, link mega-menu, prominent emergency 144.

intent-routing-layer.purpose: Page-lead affordance: five patient-intent routes (find clinic / find doctor / plan stay / international / emergency) paired with search.
intent-routing-layer.siteWideConstraint: Emergency 144 is always one of the routes; search is always present in the layer.

emergency-144.purpose: Unmistakable emergency wayfinding as the fifth intent route (red).

squaredControls.usage: primary buttons, cards, intent cards, inputs

motifs.warmPhotography: human imagery retained at captured semantic positions; intent cards may use small line icons rather than photos

voice.register: formal Swiss-German Sie; reassurance-forward; routing copy is calm and direct
dos: Do phrase the routing question warmly ('Was brauchen Sie heute?').
Do keep each intent-card description to one calm sentence.
Do label the emergency route plainly (Notfall 144).
donts: Don't use urgency/sales verbs on the routes.
Don't make the emergency route alarming for emphasis.
Don't fragment route labels across synonyms.

iaPriorities.evidence: PRODUCT.md Users lists parallel JTBD (find care / prepare stay / reassure / reach group); current IA is a flat module stack
warm grounds + warm text + human photography

brand_faithful_inversions: #ffffff retained as intent-card / card ground (brand decision). 'No pure white' inverted per brand-faithful direction.
Hex token format retained (matches captured surface). OKLCH applies to new authoring only.
Sharp 0px corners retained on intent cards/cards/inputs/primary buttons (brand counterpoint to the 18px pill). 'Modern means rounded' reflex inverted.
Saturated #0094d4 retained as primary action color. Tinted-neutral-primary reflex inverted.
#f43a11 retained as a reserved color for the emergency route only. Spread-accent reflex inverted.
divergence.antiToolboxAudit: intent-card layer audited against the SaaS feature-card-triplet anti-pattern — distinguished by being wayfinding routes (five, intent-led, one is emergency) not marketing features; passes.

components.name: Intent Card
components.description: The page-lead patient-intent route. One of five.
components.html: Klinik findenFinden Sie eine Klinik in Ihrer Nähe.
components.css: .ds-intent { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; background: #fff; color: #534c46; padding: 24px 24px 28px; border-radius: 0; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); text-decoration: none; font-family: Metropolis, sans-serif; transition: background 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-intent:hover { background: #fcfbfa; box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-intent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0094d4; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-intent-title { font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.375rem; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-intent-desc { font-weight: 400; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #72665b; }

components.name: Emergency Intent Card
components.description: The fifth route — emergency, in reserved red.
components.css: .ds-intent--emergency .ds-intent-number { font-family: Metropolis, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 2rem; line-height: 1.1; color: #f43a11; } .ds-intent--emergency:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f43a11; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Affordance
components.html: <form class="ds-search"><input class="ds-search-input" type="search" placeholder="Stichwort, Klinik oder Arzt…"><button class="ds-search-btn">Suche</button></form>
components.css: .ds-search { display: flex; background: #fff; border-radius: 18px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .ds-search-input { flex: 1; border: 1px solid #e2dfdb; border-right: none; padding: 14px 16px; font-family: Metropolis, sans-serif; color: #534c46; } .ds-search-btn { background: #0094d4; color: #fff; border: none; padding: 14px 28px; font-weight: 500; }

narrative.overview: Variant B amplifies audience-routing: the first viewport reframes around explicit patient intent — five need-cards (find clinic / find doctor / plan stay / international / emergency 144) paired with search — collapsing the captured flat module stack into one intent-organized layer.
keyCharacteristics: Module stack collapses into one organized layer
Emergency 144 is one of five routes, in red
Same pinned brand DNA; IA moves not surface

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Blue marks the four wayfinding actions; it does not decorate card grounds.

rules.name: The Reserved-Red Rule
rules.body: The Emergency 144 card is the only place #f43a11 appears.

rules.name: The Single-Shadow Rule
rules.body: The five intent cards share one shadow so the layer reads as one choice.

dos: Do lead with the intent-routing layer.
Do keep search first-viewport inside the layer.
Do make Emergency 144 one of the five routes.
donts: Don't turn routes into a marketing feature-card triplet.
Don't spread blue across card grounds.
Don't add scroll choreography or any cinematic motion register.
